findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/GroupWithZero/Finset.lean:48` has `Set.indicator_pi_one_apply`: `((s : Set ι).pi t).indicator 1 f = ∏ i ∈ s, (t i).indicator 1 (f i)`, and `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/Pi.lean:81-94` has `prod_indicator_apply`/`prod_indicator`; the PR reproves the same case manually with `Finset.prod_eq_one`/`prod_eq_zero` at lines 60-68.
findings.fix: Rewrite this as a thin wrapper using `Set.indicator_pi_one_apply` or `Finset.prod_indicator_apply` after composing with `fun ω i => X (k i) ω`, or remove the standalone theorem and use the Mathlib lemma directly in the integral proof.
findings.issue: `indicator_product_eq_indicator_rectangle_preimage` is a special case of Mathlib's product indicator lemmas, creating a parallel API for the same finite indicator-product identity.

summary: The block-law rectangle bridge itself appears new, but the product-of-indicators bridge duplicates existing Mathlib indicator-product API instead of reusing it.
